L1-EMP-01.do_en: As a DevRel engineer at a foundation-model company, you explain the open-source robot learning stack. You write tutorials, run demos, and run the community for LeRobot, GR00T, and Isaac. You collect developer feedback and pass it to product teams.
L1-EMP-01.week_en: Mon: clear community issues and answer Discord questions. Tue/Wed: write tutorials or record and publish demos. Thu: livestream and answer questions online. Fri: collect developer feedback for product and model teams, update docs.
L1-EMP-01.entry_time_en: 3-6 months to build a public portfolio (tutorials/PRs) before hiring
L1-EMP-01.barrier_en: Needs a public portfolio of content and contributions. Needs English communication. Usually requires prior open-source contributions.
L1-EMP-01.risk_en: LLMs auto-generate docs and tutorials, cutting demand. The robot developer market is still small.
L1-EMP-01.next_en: 12-24 months to DevRel lead, product marketing manager, or independent technical content business (see L1-SOLO-03).

L1-EMP-02.do_en: At customer sites, you install, tune, and run foundation-model robot systems. You integrate hardware, network, and software, write custom code for client needs, and debug. The role accepts relevant technical experience, not a required PhD.
L1-EMP-02.week_en: Mon: align with customer schedules. Tue-Thu: install, calibrate, collect data, and tune policies on site. Fri: send back failure cases and data, submit adaptation PRs, write deployment reports.
L1-EMP-02.barrier_en: Needs provable engineering project experience. Often requires travel. Needs geographic closeness to customers.
L1-EMP-02.risk_en: If foundation models become truly plug-and-play, demand for deployment labor drops.
L1-EMP-02.next_en: 12-24 months to deployment lead, solutions architect, or micro-startup offering deployment services (L5-MICRO-01).

L1-MICRO-01.do_en: A team of 1-5 builds tools or SaaS on top of open-source VLA (LeRobot/OpenPI/GR00T). It offers one-click fine-tuning and teleop-to-policy conversion. It sells to small and mid teams who want to train their own robots.
L1-MICRO-01.week_en: Mon: review user feedback and set priorities. Tue-Thu: build training and teleop features, fix pipelines. Fri: ship releases, write changelogs, run user demos and onboarding.
L1-MICRO-01.entry_time_en: 3-6 months to build a usable MVP
L1-MICRO-01.barrier_en: Needs VLA training engineering skill plus seed users. Cloud GPU cost.
L1-MICRO-01.risk_en: If Hugging Face or NVIDIA official tools build in the same features, you are crushed.
L1-MICRO-01.next_en: 12-24 months to a YC/seed-funded data and training platform company.

L1-MICRO-02.do_en: A team of 1-5 collects teleop data and fine-tunes usable VLA policies (OpenVLA/pi0/SmolVLA) per contract. It delivers evaluation reports. This is a service, not a product.
L1-MICRO-02.week_en: Mon: take requirements and define tasks. Tue-Wed: collect data. Thu: train and evaluate. Fri: deliver reports and weights, take in the next batch of tasks.
L1-MICRO-02.entry_time_en: 2-4 months (assemble collection gear plus training process)
L1-MICRO-02.barrier_en: Needs collection hardware, GPUs, and evaluation methodology.
L1-MICRO-02.risk_en: Falling data collection costs plus rising customer in-house ability squeeze margins.
L1-MICRO-02.next_en: 12-24 months to a data and policy company focused on one vertical, such as logistics.

L1-SOLO-01.do_en: You take jobs on Upwork or Toptal. You fine-tune open-source VLA (OpenVLA/pi0) with LoRA/PEFT for clients. You clean LeRobot datasets, train on a single card, and evaluate.
L1-SOLO-01.week_en: Mon: bid and align with clients. Tue-Thu: clean data, run fine-tuning and evaluation. Fri: deliver weights and eval reports, review pricing.
L1-SOLO-01.entry_time_en: 1-3 months (build an Upwork profile plus 1-2 public cases)
L1-SOLO-01.barrier_en: Needs demonstrable fine-tuning cases. Needs prepaid cloud GPU. Platform takes a cut.
L1-SOLO-01.risk_en: Automated fine-tuning tools lower the barrier. A surge in AI freelance supply drives down prices.
L1-SOLO-01.next_en: 12-24 months to a VLA fine-tuning studio (L1-MICRO-02) or an expert consultant brand.

L1-SOLO-02.name_en: Independent Embodied AI Researcher / LeRobot Contributor
L1-SOLO-02.do_en: You publish LeRobot models and datasets on Hugging Face Hub. You do open research. You earn through bounties, sponsorships, and grants, building academic and technical reputation.
L1-SOLO-02.week_en: Mon-Wed: run experiments, train models, collect data. Thu: write and publish model cards and blogs. Fri: engage the community, apply for bounties and grants, maintain sponsor pages.
L1-SOLO-02.barrier_en: Needs a self-bought entry robot arm plus GPU. Self-discipline and slow reputation building.
L1-SOLO-02.risk_en: Open research is hard to monetize. Big-company open source crowds out individual contribution visibility.
L1-SOLO-02.next_en: 12-24 months to recruitment by a lab (L1-EMP) or seed funding to build tools (L1-MICRO-01).

L1-SOLO-03.name_en: Robotics / Embodied AI Educator and Content Creator
L1-SOLO-03.do_en: You make teaching content for developers across levels. Topics span VLA model explainers, SO-ARM/LeRobot builds, and ROS2 courses. You earn through YouTube ads, Udemy courses, and sponsorships.
L1-SOLO-03.week_en: Mon: pick topics and write scripts. Tue-Wed: record model explainers, robot builds, or ROS2 lessons. Thu: edit and publish. Fri: reply to comments, update courses, negotiate sponsorships.
L1-SOLO-03.entry_time_en: 6-12 months to grow an audience large enough to monetize
L1-SOLO-03.barrier_en: Needs camera, microphone, and demo hardware. Slow audience growth and high dropout rate.
L1-SOLO-03.risk_en: AI-generated teaching content floods the market. Platform algorithm and revenue-share changes.
L1-SOLO-03.next_en: 12-24 months to a paid community or bootcamp, or co-branded sales with hardware makers.

L1-SOLO-04.name_en: Embodied Red Team / VLA Evaluation Benchmark Builder
L1-SOLO-04.do_en: You focus on adversarial testing and reliability testing of embodied models. You build adversarial instructions and evaluation harnesses. You run safety assessments for labs and companies by contract or bounty.
L1-SOLO-04.week_en: Mon-Wed: design and run adversarial evaluations, build harnesses. Thu: organize findings and write reports. Fri: submit bounties, line up eval contracts, update public benchmarks.
L1-SOLO-04.barrier_en: Needs solid evaluation and safety methodology. The market is still early.
L1-SOLO-04.risk_en: The embodied safety market is immature. Demand depends on regulatory push.
L1-SOLO-04.next_en: 12-24 months to an embodied AI safety company or recruitment by a frontier lab.

L2-EMP-01.do_en: Build data pipelines for simulation and synthetic data firms. This covers sensor simulation, domain randomization, and auto-generated labeled training data. The data feeds client perception and manipulation models.
L2-EMP-01.week_en: Monday: align on data requirements. Tuesday to Thursday: build and tune pipelines, then generate and quality-check datasets. Friday: deliver data and tune generation parameters.
L2-EMP-01.entry_time_en: 3-6 months with a data or Python engineering background.
L2-EMP-01.barrier_en: Requires software engineering skill, though a PhD is not required. Competition is intense.
L2-EMP-01.risk_en: Generative world models may replace hand-built synthetic data pipelines.
L2-EMP-01.next_en: 12-24 months to senior simulation platform engineer, or freelance synthetic data contracting (L2-SOLO-01).

L2-EMP-02.do_en: Build robot simulation environments and engine features in Unreal, Unity, or Omniverse. This work supports sim-to-real training and validation.
L2-EMP-02.week_en: Monday: plan the schedule. Tuesday to Thursday: build engine and environment features, then fix bugs. Friday: code review and integration testing.
L2-EMP-02.entry_time_en: 3-6 months with an engine or game programming background.
L2-EMP-02.barrier_en: Requires strong C++ and engine skills. Moving from games is feasible.
L2-EMP-02.risk_en: Engine vendor tools plus AI-assisted coding cut headcount needs.
L2-EMP-02.next_en: 12-24 months to simulation team lead, or selling simulation assets solo (L2-SOLO-02).

L2-EMP-03.do_en: Deploy Isaac Sim, Omniverse, and digital twin solutions for clients and partners. Build scenes, run integrations, and deliver enablement demos. The role leans toward DevRel plus solutions.
L2-EMP-03.week_en: Monday: gather client requirements. Tuesday to Thursday: build demo scenes and integrate robot algorithms. Friday: run client enablement and feed feedback to NVIDIA product lines.
L2-EMP-03.entry_time_en: 3-9 months to master the Omniverse and USD stack.
L2-EMP-03.barrier_en: Requires Omniverse and USD expertise plus client communication skills.
L2-EMP-03.risk_en: Demand falls if the Omniverse environment contracts.
L2-EMP-03.next_en: 12-24 months to solutions manager, or independent Omniverse consultant.

L2-MICRO-01.name_en: World Model / Simulation Environment Micro-Startup (Policy Evaluation)
L2-MICRO-01.do_en: A team of 1-5 builds world models or simulation environments. These train and evaluate robot policies, such as cloth or packing tasks. Sell access or evaluation services.
L2-MICRO-01.week_en: Monday: set R&D priorities. Tuesday to Thursday: build simulation tasks and evaluation pipelines. Friday: meet clients and investors, then ship a demo.
L2-MICRO-01.barrier_en: High technical bar. Mostly venture-driven with no early revenue.
L2-MICRO-01.revenue_src_en: Seed funding and early contracts (founder salary)
L2-MICRO-01.risk_en: Large-vendor world models like NVIDIA Cosmos squeeze the space. Technical risk is high.
L2-MICRO-01.next_en: 12-24 months to a funded simulation or world model company via a seed round.

L2-MICRO-02.name_en: Manufacturing Synthetic Data + Simulation CV Micro-Startup
L2-MICRO-02.do_en: A team of 1-5 builds synthetic data and simulation CV products for manufacturing. Clients shoot less real data while still training defect detection and grasping models.
L2-MICRO-02.week_en: Monday: meet clients. Tuesday to Thursday: generate data, train models, and deploy edge CV. Friday: run client acceptance and iterate.
L2-MICRO-02.barrier_en: Requires CV plus manufacturing know-how and early clients.
L2-MICRO-02.risk_en: General CV and foundation models erode demand for custom synthetic data.
L2-MICRO-02.next_en: 12-24 months to a vertical manufacturing AI inspection company.

L2-SOLO-01.do_en: Take jobs on Upwork to build simulation environments, model URDF, and run navigation or manipulation simulation. Also handle synthetic data pipelines.
L2-SOLO-01.week_en: Monday: submit bids. Tuesday to Thursday: build simulations, model, and tune parameters. Friday: deliver, record a demo video, and review.
L2-SOLO-01.entry_time_en: 1-3 months to build a profile and case studies.
L2-SOLO-01.barrier_en: Requires a portfolio. Platform takes a cut. Needs a strong GPU machine.
L2-SOLO-01.risk_en: AI assistance lowers the bar and cuts prices. Simulation-as-a-service platforms emerge.
L2-SOLO-01.next_en: 12-24 months to a simulation studio, or joining a platform (L2-EMP).

L2-SOLO-02.name_en: SimReady / USD Digital Twin 3D Asset Seller
L2-SOLO-02.do_en: Build and sell physics-ready SimReady USD assets and scenes on Omniverse, Unreal Fab, and Unity Asset Store. Robot teams buy them to build simulations. Income is passive plus custom work.
L2-SOLO-02.week_en: Monday to Wednesday: model and add physics and semantic properties. Thursday: publish and write docs. Friday: check sales, do custom orders, and update popular assets.
L2-SOLO-02.entry_time_en: 1-3 months to ship a first batch of assets.
L2-SOLO-02.barrier_en: Requires 3D modeling skill. Long-tail passive income ramps slowly.
L2-SOLO-02.risk_en: Generative 3D, where text creates assets, disrupts the hand-built asset market.
L2-SOLO-02.next_en: 12-24 months to a digital twin asset studio, or large-vendor custom contracts.

L2-SOLO-03.name_en: MuJoCo / Isaac Lab Plugin and Sim-to-Real Developer
L2-SOLO-03.do_en: Maintain MuJoCo and Isaac Lab plugins and RL sim-to-real tools on GitHub. Monetize through GitHub Sponsors, bounties, and consulting.
L2-SOLO-03.week_en: Monday to Wednesday: develop plugins and handle issues and PRs. Thursday: release and write docs. Friday: take consulting jobs and maintain the sponsor page.
L2-SOLO-03.barrier_en: Requires deep simulation and RL skill. Sponsor income ramps slowly.
L2-SOLO-03.risk_en: Official tools absorb plugin features. Pure open-source monetization is hard.
L2-SOLO-03.next_en: 12-24 months to recruitment by a simulation company, or building a paid tool.

L3-SOLO-01-WEST.do_en: You use personal VR or SO-ARM gear at home to teleoperate robots. You record demonstration data and get paid per demo. This data trains robot models.
L3-SOLO-01-WEST.week_en: Monday you pick up the task list. Tuesday to Friday you teleoperate remotely and collect demos. You self-check pass rates and submit for payment.
L3-SOLO-01-WEST.entry_time_en: Several days to two weeks (gear setup plus training)
L3-SOLO-01-WEST.barrier_en: Requires VR/teleoperation gear and stable internet. Piece-rate income is volatile.
L3-SOLO-01-WEST.risk_en: Simulated data or low-cost overseas collection may replace this. Automated data generation is a further threat.
L3-SOLO-01-WEST.next_en: In 12-24 months, move to a teleoperation data studio (L3-MICRO-01-WEST) or become a team lead.

L3-SOLO-01-CN.do_en: You work on-site at a data collection factory. You wear teleoperation or exoskeleton gear and repeatedly collect robot demonstration data. Pay is per session or per work hour.
L3-SOLO-01-CN.week_en: You follow the factory shift schedule. Each day you repeat several sessions of the same task type. Each session runs about three hours, with on-site checks before payment.
L3-SOLO-01-CN.barrier_en: Requires a nearby data factory. Pay is low with repetitive strain risk.
L3-SOLO-01-CN.risk_en: Collection automation or simulation may replace this work. Regional overcapacity also pushes prices down.
L3-SOLO-01-CN.next_en: In 12-24 months, become a data factory team lead or operations role (L3-MICRO-01-CN).

L3-SOLO-02.name_en: Robot Demonstration Dataset Creator and Seller
L3-SOLO-02.do_en: You record demonstration datasets with your own SO-ARM100 or ALOHA. You sell them per set or by license on the Hugging Face Hub or data markets like DeepAware. You may also earn bounties.
L3-SOLO-02.week_en: Monday you pick high-demand tasks. Tuesday to Thursday you record, check, and package. Friday you list, price, set licenses, and follow up with buyers.
L3-SOLO-02.entry_time_en: Two to four weeks (assemble gear plus first dataset)
L3-SOLO-02.barrier_en: Requires buying your own robot arm. Market demand and pricing are unstable.
L3-SOLO-02.risk_en: A surge in dataset supply pushes prices down. Large firms may build their own data.
L3-SOLO-02.next_en: In 12-24 months, move to a micro data studio (L3-MICRO-01).

L3-SOLO-03.do_en: You wear a head camera or phone to record first-person operation videos. You collect these in homes, factories, or retail settings. Pay is hourly or per task, feeding embodied learning.
L3-SOLO-03.week_en: You complete daily tasks at assigned sites per your dispatch. You record first-person video, upload it the same day, and get paid after quality checks.
L3-SOLO-03.barrier_en: Limited by regional availability. Hourly pay is low, especially in emerging markets.
L3-SOLO-03.risk_en: Ample supply keeps unit prices low. Demand drops once data saturates.
L3-SOLO-03.next_en: In 12-24 months, become a collection team supervisor (L3-MICRO-03).

L3-SOLO-04.do_en: You remotely operate robots or street rovers through decentralized protocols to earn tokens and points. PrismaX combines staking with teleoperation. FrodoBots pays for driving. Earnings depend on tokens not yet launched.
L3-SOLO-04.week_en: Each day you complete teleoperation or driving tasks to earn points and quick rebates. You manage staking and wallets and watch TGE progress.
L3-SOLO-04.entry_time_en: Several days (sign-up plus gear or rental car)
L3-SOLO-04.barrier_en: Tokens are pre-TGE, so cash value is highly uncertain. Gear or car rental adds cost.
L3-SOLO-04.risk_en: Tokens may go to zero or the project may fail. Regulation and reward dilution are further risks.
L3-SOLO-04.next_en: In 12-24 months, run a teleoperation guild (L3-MICRO-04).

L3-SOLO-05.do_en: You annotate and verify data on a blockchain data protocol like Codatta. This includes robot trajectories and address metadata. You earn points and ongoing royalties based on quality scores.
L3-SOLO-05.week_en: Each day you take annotation tasks, submit them, and join consensus verification. You build up points and royalties and track token activity.
L3-SOLO-05.entry_time_en: A few hours (start right after sign-up)
L3-SOLO-05.barrier_en: Returns are highly uncertain. Token value is low and earnings depend on speculative tokens.
L3-SOLO-05.risk_en: Tokens may go to zero or reward pools may dilute. The protocol may fail.
L3-SOLO-05.next_en: In 12-24 months, run an annotation community or build royalty-bearing assets.

L3-SOLO-06.do_en: You use Rokoko or Xsens mocap suits and gloves to capture human demonstration motions. You take jobs on Fiverr or Upwork. You clean and export the data for robots or animation.
L3-SOLO-06.week_en: Monday you take jobs and talk with clients. Tuesday to Thursday you calibrate, capture, and clean data. Friday you deliver, review, and maintain your portfolio.
L3-SOLO-06.entry_time_en: Two to four weeks (gear setup plus sample reel)
L3-SOLO-06.barrier_en: A mocap suit and gloves require notable starting capital.
L3-SOLO-06.risk_en: Markerless video mocap using pure computer vision may replace suits.
L3-SOLO-06.next_en: In 12-24 months, run a motion-capture-as-a-service studio (L3-MICRO-02).

L3-SOLO-07.do_en: You take robot multimodal data jobs on platforms like Upwork. These cover video, depth, and force annotation plus quality checks. Pay is hourly or per piece.
L3-SOLO-07.week_en: Monday you take jobs. Tuesday to Friday you annotate in batches and self-check. Friday you settle pay and seek long-term clients.
L3-SOLO-07.barrier_en: Low entry bar leads to heavy competition and price pressure.
L3-SOLO-07.risk_en: Auto-annotation models may replace basic annotation. Low-price competition is a further threat.
L3-SOLO-07.next_en: In 12-24 months, become an annotation QC lead (L3-EMP-03) or run a micro annotation shop (L3-MICRO-05).

L3-MICRO-01-WEST.do_en: A team of one to five runs several SO-ARM or teleoperation stations. You collect demonstration data in batches under contract. You sell it to labs and data companies.
L3-MICRO-01-WEST.week_en: Monday you take contracts and set tasks. Tuesday to Thursday you collect across stations and check quality. Friday you deliver batches, reconcile, and pitch new deals.
L3-MICRO-01-WEST.entry_time_en: One to three months (gear, process, plus first deal)
L3-MICRO-01-WEST.barrier_en: Requires multiple gear sets and steady clients. Cash flow comes up front.
L3-MICRO-01-WEST.risk_en: Low-cost overseas collection plus simulation may replace this. Clients may build in-house.
L3-MICRO-01-WEST.next_en: In 12-24 months, grow into a regional data collection company.

L3-MICRO-01-CN.do_en: You run a physical data collection site with dozens of stations. You organize workers to collect teleoperation and demonstration data in bulk. You sell it per set to robot companies, at larger scale and lower price than Western studios.
L3-MICRO-01-CN.week_en: You manage multiple worker shifts collecting data. You quality-check and store it per SOP. You deliver large batches to robot firms under contract.
L3-MICRO-01-CN.entry_time_en: Three to nine months (site, gear, plus staffing)
L3-MICRO-01-CN.barrier_en: High capital, site, and staffing needs. This is a small business, not a solo role.
L3-MICRO-01-CN.risk_en: Regional overcapacity pushes prices down. Collection automation is a further threat.
L3-MICRO-01-CN.next_en: In 12-24 months, become a leading regional data supplier.

L3-MICRO-02.do_en: A team of one to five runs its own Rokoko or Xsens mocap stage. You take custom human motion capture and cleanup jobs. You serve robot, game, and film clients.
L3-MICRO-02.week_en: Monday and Tuesday you schedule and set up the stage. Wednesday and Thursday you capture multiple projects. Friday you clean, deliver, and pitch new projects.
L3-MICRO-02.entry_time_en: One to three months (stage, sample reel, plus clients)
L3-MICRO-02.barrier_en: Requires gear and studio space capital plus client development.
L3-MICRO-02.risk_en: Markerless video mocap lowers demand for studio stages.
L3-MICRO-02.next_en: In 12-24 months, grow into a regional mocap or data service company.

L3-MICRO-03.name_en: Egocentric Collection Team Operations / Field Supervisor
L3-MICRO-03.do_en: You recruit and manage a team of gig workers wearing cameras. You organize bulk first-person data collection in factories, homes, and retail. You earn the service margin on volume.
L3-MICRO-03.week_en: You recruit and schedule workers and deploy them to sites. You run quality checks and manage uploads. You settle by 4K video volume and grow the team.
L3-MICRO-03.entry_time_en: One to three months (build team plus first contracts)
L3-MICRO-03.barrier_en: Requires a labor network and field management. Thin unit price relies on volume.
L3-MICRO-03.risk_en: Demand drops once data saturates. Platforms may hire directly and bypass middlemen.
L3-MICRO-03.next_en: In 12-24 months, grow into a regional data collection service company.

L3-MICRO-04.do_en: You form and stake a teleoperation guild on PrismaX. You recruit and dispatch operators. You take a share of the points and quick rebates they produce. Earnings depend on tokens not yet launched.
L3-MICRO-04.week_en: You recruit and train operators and manage staking and task routing. You monitor output and rewards. You distribute earnings and grow headcount.
L3-MICRO-04.barrier_en: Tokens are pre-TGE, so returns are uncertain. Requires community and staked capital.
L3-MICRO-04.risk_en: Tokens may go to zero or the project may fail. Regulation is a further risk.
L3-MICRO-04.next_en: In 12-24 months, operate a decentralized data network.

L3-MICRO-05.do_en: A team of one to five runs a small annotation shop. You take subcontracted robot multimodal annotation and quality checks from data platforms. You handle delivery and staff training.
L3-MICRO-05.week_en: Monday you take subcontracts and set the SOP. Tuesday to Thursday you organize annotation and quality checks. Friday you deliver, reconcile, and train new annotators.
L3-MICRO-05.entry_time_en: One to two months (build team plus first deal)
L3-MICRO-05.barrier_en: Requires client subcontract relationships. Margins are thin.
L3-MICRO-05.risk_en: Auto-annotation may replace this. Low-price regional competition is a further threat.
L3-MICRO-05.next_en: In 12-24 months, grow into a regional annotation service company or specialize in one modality.

L3-EMP-01.do_en: You work as a salaried operator at a teleoperation-as-a-service company. You use VR or XR to remotely control robots. You collect data or handle edge cases.
L3-EMP-01.week_en: You teleoperate robots by shift to collect data or take over. You log sessions, handle exceptions, and hand off cleanly.
L3-EMP-01.entry_time_en: Several days to two weeks (onboarding training)
L3-EMP-01.barrier_en: Requires stable internet and shift work. Some roles require on-site presence.
L3-EMP-01.risk_en: More autonomy reduces human takeover. Offshore low-wage competition is a further threat.
L3-EMP-01.next_en: In 12-24 months, become an operations supervisor or data operations role (L3-EMP-02).

L3-EMP-02.name_en: Data Operations Manager / Human Data Strategist
L3-EMP-02.do_en: You manage annotation projects at a data company. You improve data quality, train annotators, and work with clients. You ensure robot data is delivered.
L3-EMP-02.week_en: Monday you set the project plan. Tuesday to Thursday you monitor quality, coordinate annotators, and align with clients. Friday you review metrics and improve SOPs.
L3-EMP-02.entry_time_en: Zero to three months (with operations or PM experience)
L3-EMP-02.barrier_en: Requires PM or operations experience. This is not an entry-level role.
L3-EMP-02.risk_en: Annotation automation may shrink team size.
L3-EMP-02.next_en: In 12-24 months, become a data operations director or run your own annotation shop (L3-MICRO-05).

L3-EMP-03.do_en: You work as a salaried annotator at a data engine company. You review teleoperation trajectories and multi-view video. You label the data.
L3-EMP-03.week_en: You review and annotate video and trajectories by queue. You label per the spec, self-check, and meet daily output targets.
L3-EMP-03.barrier_en: Low entry bar. Pay is on the low side.
L3-EMP-03.next_en: In 12-24 months, become an annotation QC lead or data operations role (L3-EMP-02).

L3-EMP-04.do_en: You recruit, screen, and train annotators and operators for a data company. You grow the global network of data contributors.
L3-EMP-04.week_en: Monday you set recruiting targets. Tuesday to Thursday you source, interview, and onboard. Friday you review retention and capacity and tune the funnel.
L3-EMP-04.risk_en: AI auto-recruiting, such as micro1 Zara, may replace some human work.
L3-EMP-04.next_en: In 12-24 months, become a community or operations lead.

L3-EMP-05.do_en: You build infrastructure for robot multimodal data platforms. This covers data ingestion, retrieval, visualization, and ML pipeline tools.
L3-EMP-05.week_en: Monday you plan the sprint. Tuesday to Thursday you build data platform features and tools. Friday you review, ship, and support internal users.
L3-EMP-05.entry_time_en: Three to six months (with a data engineering background)
L3-EMP-05.barrier_en: Requires software engineering skills, but a PhD is not required.
L3-EMP-05.risk_en: General data platforms may replace purpose-built tools.
L3-EMP-05.next_en: In 12-24 months, become a senior or platform lead, or move to L5 middleware.

L4-EMP-01.name_en: Humanoid Robot Operator / Teleoperation Data Pilot
L4-EMP-01.do_en: Wear teleoperation or motion-capture gear to drive humanoid robots and collect training data. Follow set procedures, log failures, and upload sessions. A high school diploma is enough to start.
L4-EMP-01.week_en: Each shift, wear the gear and guide robots through behaviors for data collection. Label each session. File failure tickets. Set up the work environment.
L4-EMP-01.entry_time_en: A few days to two weeks of training
L4-EMP-01.barrier_en: Requires a nearby lab. Physical stamina needed. Most work is on-site.
L4-EMP-01.risk_en: Autonomy and simulated data may cut the need for on-site collection.
L4-EMP-01.next_en: In 12-24 months, move to operations supervisor, data operations, or test technician (L4-EMP-02).

L4-EMP-02.do_en: Assemble robot subassemblies by procedure. Run powered tests and hardware QA using oscilloscopes and multimeters. Record defects. No PhD required.
L4-EMP-02.week_en: Assemble subassemblies from work orders. Run bring-up tests and QA. Record and report defects. Support engineering iterations.
L4-EMP-02.entry_time_en: A few weeks of on-the-job training
L4-EMP-02.barrier_en: Requires a nearby factory. Hands-on ability needed.
L4-EMP-02.risk_en: Production lines automate. Mass production may move to low-cost regions.
L4-EMP-02.next_en: In 12-24 months, move to test engineer, line supervisor, or field service (L4-EMP-03).

L4-EMP-03.do_en: Go to customer sites to install, calibrate, diagnose, and repair robots such as Spot and Stretch. Provide customer support.
L4-EMP-03.week_en: Follow dispatch orders to sites. Install, calibrate, diagnose, and repair. Train customers. Report issues and spare-part needs.
L4-EMP-03.entry_time_en: 0-3 months with electrical or mechanical experience
L4-EMP-03.risk_en: Remote and self-diagnosis may reduce on-site demand.
L4-EMP-03.next_en: In 12-24 months, move to field service supervisor or application engineer.

L4-EMP-04.name_en: Hardware Application / Integration Engineer (Dexterous Hands / Sensors)
L4-EMP-04.do_en: Integrate hardware like dexterous hands and tactile sensors into customer systems. Provide application support. A GitHub portfolio can replace a PhD.
L4-EMP-04.week_en: Monday, align on customer integration needs. Tuesday to Thursday, integrate hardware and write examples. Friday, support questions and feed back improvements.
L4-EMP-04.barrier_en: Requires demonstrable hardware or integration projects.
L4-EMP-04.risk_en: Plug-and-play hardware may lower demand for integration support.
L4-EMP-04.next_en: In 12-24 months, move to application engineering lead or solutions engineer.

L4-MICRO-01-WEST.name_en: SO-ARM/LeRobot Kit Assembly, Resale, and Bundling
L4-MICRO-01-WEST.do_en: A team of one to five sources parts, 3D-prints structural pieces, and assembles ready-to-use SO-ARM/LeRobot kits in Western markets. They run QA, sell kits and bundles, and offer beginner support.
L4-MICRO-01-WEST.week_en: Monday, stock parts and process orders. Tuesday to Thursday, assemble, QA, and ship. Friday, handle support, content marketing, and restocking.
L4-MICRO-01-WEST.entry_time_en: 1-3 months for supply, prototype, and store
L4-MICRO-01-WEST.barrier_en: Requires stock capital and assembly capacity. Logistics and returns add load.
L4-MICRO-01-WEST.risk_en: Large makers may mass-produce open-source designs cheaply. Margins are thin.
L4-MICRO-01-WEST.next_en: In 12-24 months, build an own-brand education robot company.

L4-MICRO-01-CN.name_en: SO-ARM/LeRobot Kit and Parts Manufacturing and Resale (Shenzhen Supply Chain)
L4-MICRO-01-CN.do_en: Using the Shenzhen supply chain along Seeed Studio and Waveshare routes, manufacture and sell full SO-ARM kits and parts. Win on cost and capacity, selling worldwide.
L4-MICRO-01-CN.week_en: Organize manufacturing and inspection of parts and full units. Run cross-border stores and distribution. Ship by volume and iterate the design.
L4-MICRO-01-CN.entry_time_en: 1-3 months to set up the supply chain
L4-MICRO-01-CN.barrier_en: Requires supply chain relationships and stock capital.
L4-MICRO-01-CN.risk_en: Homogenized price wars hurt margins. Large makers crush on scale.
L4-MICRO-01-CN.next_en: In 12-24 months, build an open-source robot hardware brand or ODM.

L4-MICRO-02.do_en: Sell 3D-printed SO-ARM/LeRobot structural parts or digital STL files on Etsy, Tindie, and MakerWorld. Designs come from The Robot Studio open-source CAD.
L4-MICRO-02.week_en: Monday, take orders and prepare materials. Tuesday to Thursday, print, post-process, and ship. Friday, list new items and STLs, and handle support.
L4-MICRO-02.entry_time_en: A few weeks for a printer and first batch
L4-MICRO-02.barrier_en: Requires a 3D printer. Physical-part profit is limited by print time.
L4-MICRO-02.risk_en: Anyone can print open-source parts. Price pressure is high.
L4-MICRO-02.next_en: In 12-24 months, upgrade to full kit resale (L4-MICRO-01-WEST).

L4-MICRO-03.do_en: Design and sell accessories and modular mounting kits for robots like Unitree Go2 and G1. Kits include Picatinny rails and payload mounts. A team of one to five does small-batch production.
L4-MICRO-03.week_en: Monday to Tuesday, design and iterate accessories. Wednesday to Thursday, produce and ship. Friday, list, market, and handle support.
L4-MICRO-03.entry_time_en: 1-2 months for design and first batch
L4-MICRO-03.barrier_en: Requires design and manufacturing skill. The niche market is small.
L4-MICRO-03.risk_en: Platform models may change or end. Knockoff competition exists.
L4-MICRO-03.next_en: In 12-24 months, build a robot accessory brand or distribution.

L4-SOLO-01.do_en: Take robot and humanoid concept and part jobs on Upwork and Cad Crowd. Work covers CAD design, reverse engineering, rendering, and print-ready DFM.
L4-SOLO-01.week_en: Monday, bid on jobs. Tuesday to Thursday, produce CAD, render, and revise. Friday, deliver, review pricing, and update the portfolio.
L4-SOLO-01.entry_time_en: 1-3 months for a profile and case work
L4-SOLO-01.barrier_en: Requires a CAD portfolio. Platforms take a cut.
L4-SOLO-01.risk_en: AI-assisted CAD and generative design may push prices down.
L4-SOLO-01.next_en: In 12-24 months, start a robot design studio or join a hardware company.

L4-SOLO-02.name_en: Open-Source Hardware Kit Community / Developer Relations (Contract)
L4-SOLO-02.do_en: Do community and developer relations on contract for open-source hardware makers. Run Discord, write integration guides, and demo at events. Earn sponsorships, affiliate income, and contract fees.
L4-SOLO-02.week_en: Monday, answer community questions. Tuesday to Wednesday, write guides and build integration demos. Thursday, run events or streams. Friday, align with makers and create affiliate content.
L4-SOLO-02.barrier_en: Requires community reputation and hardware familiarity.
L4-SOLO-02.revenue_src_en: Contract fees plus sponsorship and affiliate income
L4-SOLO-02.risk_en: Maker budgets fluctuate. Community platforms change.
L4-SOLO-02.next_en: In 12-24 months, join DevRel (L1-EMP-01) or run own content (L1-SOLO-03).

L5-EMP-01.do_en: Run customer-facing end-to-end deployments at a robotics middleware company. Bring the platform onto customer robots and fleets. Handle integration and hardware prototyping.
L5-EMP-01.week_en: Monday: meet customers. Tuesday to Thursday: integrate the platform and bring fleets online. Friday: deliver, feed back to product, and travel sometimes.
L5-EMP-01.entry_time_en: 0-3 months with a software or integration background
L5-EMP-01.barrier_en: Requires software ability. Some travel needed.
L5-EMP-01.risk_en: Platform self-service features reduce the headcount needed for deployment.
L5-EMP-01.next_en: 12-24 months to solutions lead or fleet integration consultant (L5-MICRO-01).

L5-EMP-02.do_en: Write ROS2 software covering navigation, Nav2, integration, and SDKs. Open to self-taught and bootcamp software backgrounds. No PhD required.
L5-EMP-02.week_en: Monday: plan the schedule. Tuesday to Thursday: write and debug ROS2 features and integration. Friday: review, test, and release.
L5-EMP-02.entry_time_en: 3-9 months to self-learn ROS2 to a hireable level
L5-EMP-02.barrier_en: Requires solid software ability and a portfolio. A PhD is not required.
L5-EMP-02.next_en: 12-24 months to senior, middleware lead, or freelance ROS2 developer (L5-SOLO-01).

L5-EMP-03.name_en: Robotics Technical / Application Support Engineer (AMR Fleets)
L5-EMP-03.do_en: Provide after-sales technical support for AMR fleets. Handle CI/CD deployment, troubleshooting, and customer support.
L5-EMP-03.week_en: Monday to Friday: handle customer tickets, deploy updates, and troubleshoot remotely. Escalate critical issues. Send product feedback back.
L5-EMP-03.next_en: 12-24 months to solutions or deployment engineer (L5-EMP-01).

L5-EMP-04.name_en: Robotics DevOps/SRE and Functional Safety Engineer
L5-EMP-04.do_en: Run DevOps, SRE, and functional safety for safety-critical robotics stacks. Build deployment pipelines and V&V. Support safety certification.
L5-EMP-04.week_en: Monday to Thursday: maintain deployment and CI, run V&V and safety cases, and monitor. Friday: write compliance docs and release.
L5-EMP-04.entry_time_en: 3-6 months with a DevOps or embedded background
L5-EMP-04.barrier_en: Requires DevOps skills plus safety methodology.
L5-EMP-04.risk_en: The safety market depends on regulatory progress.
L5-EMP-04.next_en: 12-24 months to safety or platform engineering lead.

L5-SOLO-01.do_en: Take ROS and ROS2 contracts on Upwork and Arc.dev. Work covers SLAM, PX4, and autonomous navigation.
L5-SOLO-01.week_en: Monday: bid on jobs. Tuesday to Thursday: write and debug ROS2 code. Friday: deliver, demo, and review.
L5-SOLO-01.entry_time_en: 1-3 months to build a profile and case studies
L5-SOLO-01.barrier_en: Requires a portfolio. Platforms take a cut.
L5-SOLO-01.risk_en: AI-assisted coding pushes prices down. Offshore competition adds pressure.
L5-SOLO-01.next_en: 12-24 months to fleet integration consultant (L5-MICRO-01) or employment (L5-EMP-02).

L5-SOLO-02.do_en: Build and publish modules or panels on the Viam Registry or Foxglove extension marketplace. Viam handles billing. Earn distribution income or consulting leads.
skills_must_en: Viam component/service API or Foxglove SDK
L5-SOLO-02.week_en: Monday to Wednesday: build and test modules or extensions. Thursday: publish and write docs. Friday: track adoption and take consulting or custom work.
L5-SOLO-02.barrier_en: Paid distribution is early-stage. Monetization is uncertain.
L5-SOLO-02.risk_en: Paid distribution mechanisms are immature. Platform policy can change.
L5-SOLO-02.next_en: 12-24 months to a vertical SaaS built on the platform (L5-MICRO-02).

L5-SOLO-03.do_en: Maintain open-source robotics middleware such as dora-rs or ROS packages. Earn through GitHub Sponsors, consulting, and bounties.
L5-SOLO-03.week_en: Monday to Wednesday: develop, maintain, and handle issues and PRs. Thursday: release. Friday: take consulting and maintain the sponsor page.
L5-SOLO-03.barrier_en: Requires systems programming skills. Sponsorship scales slowly.
L5-SOLO-03.risk_en: Pure OSS is hard to monetize. Large firms dominate the space.
L5-SOLO-03.next_en: 12-24 months to recruitment by a middleware company or a commercial project.

L5-MICRO-01.do_en: Run a 1-5 person studio deploying Formant or InOrbit fleet operations and teleoperation for customers. Do cloud robotics integration.
L5-MICRO-01.week_en: Monday: meet customers. Tuesday to Thursday: integrate and deploy fleet operations. Friday: deliver, maintain, and discuss renewals or new deals.
L5-MICRO-01.barrier_en: Requires platform expertise plus customer relationships.
L5-MICRO-01.risk_en: Platform self-service grows. Customers build their own teams.
L5-MICRO-01.next_en: 12-24 months to a regional robotics integration services firm.

L5-MICRO-02.do_en: Run a 1-5 person team building a vertical robotics SaaS app on Viam or Formant. Charge customers for it.
L5-MICRO-02.week_en: Monday: review user feedback. Tuesday to Thursday: build vertical app features. Friday: ship, onboard customers, and market.
L5-MICRO-02.entry_time_en: 3-6 months to an MVP and first customers
L5-MICRO-02.barrier_en: Requires platform plus app development skills and vertical customers.
L5-MICRO-02.risk_en: Platform policy or pricing can change. Vertical demand may be limited.
L5-MICRO-02.next_en: 12-24 months to a vertical robotics SaaS company.

L6-EMP-01.name_en: Remote Robot Operator / Takeover Intervention Specialist
L6-EMP-01.do_en: Work as a remote operator. Monitor video from warehouse picking and sidewalk delivery robots. Take control when something goes wrong. Low entry bar, with paid training included.
L6-EMP-01.week_en: Each shift, monitor video feeds from several robots. Take remote control when faults appear. Log each intervention and hand off to the next shift.
L6-EMP-01.entry_time_en: A few days (30 hours of paid training)
L6-EMP-01.barrier_en: Needs stable internet. Shift work. Some roles are on-site.
L6-EMP-01.risk_en: Higher autonomy lets one person manage more robots. This compresses the number of positions.
L6-EMP-01.next_en: 12-24 months to fleet supervisor or deployment manager (L6-EMP-03).

L6-EMP-02.name_en: Vertical Robot Field Deployment / Service Technician
L6-EMP-02.do_en: Visit customer sites to install, configure, and repair vertical-industry robots in warehousing, agriculture, and food. Run control and safety tests. Train customers. Roles often span several verticals.
L6-EMP-02.week_en: Take dispatch tickets and go on-site to install and configure robots. Run safety tests and training. Fix faults and report issues back.
L6-EMP-02.barrier_en: Needs electromechanical basics. Involves travel and on-site work.
L6-EMP-02.risk_en: Remote diagnostics and more reliable hardware reduce the need for on-site visits.
L6-EMP-02.next_en: 12-24 months to deployment manager, regional service lead, or own integration shop (L6-MICRO-01).

L6-EMP-03.do_en: Own RaaS customer accounts. Manage deployments and analyze results. Drive expansion and repeat purchases. Coordinate across departments.
L6-EMP-03.week_en: Monday, set customer success plans. Tuesday to Thursday, track deployment results, align with customers, and push expansion. Friday, review metrics and coordinate internally.
L6-EMP-03.barrier_en: Needs customer success or deployment experience.
L6-EMP-03.risk_en: RaaS demand swings with customer budgets.
L6-EMP-03.next_en: 12-24 months to customer success director or regional lead.

L6-EMP-04.do_en: Work as an employee doing drone mapping and data capture on construction and infrastructure sites. Flights are autonomous. Holds an FAA Part 107 certificate.
L6-EMP-04.week_en: For each project, go on-site and plan flight routes. Fly to capture data. Process the data and deliver the results.
L6-EMP-04.barrier_en: Needs a Part 107 certificate. Involves on-site work and travel.
L6-EMP-04.risk_en: Fully autonomous drones reduce demand for human pilots.
L6-EMP-04.next_en: 12-24 months to own drone service (L6-SOLO-01) or a service company (L6-MICRO-03).

L6-SOLO-01.name_en: Part 107 Drone Inspection / Mapping Solo Operator
L6-SOLO-01.do_en: Hold a Part 107 certificate and fly Skydio or DJI drones. Provide inspection and mapping for construction, infrastructure, and agriculture. Bill by hour, project, or acre.
L6-SOLO-01.week_en: Monday, take orders and plan. Tuesday to Thursday, fly on-site and capture data. Friday, process data, deliver reports, and find clients.
L6-SOLO-01.risk_en: Fully autonomous inspection platforms emerge. A saturated market pushes prices down.
L6-SOLO-01.next_en: 12-24 months to a drone service company (L6-MICRO-03).

L6-SOLO-02.do_en: Own or rent laser or mechanical weeding robots. Offer weeding-as-a-service to farms. Bill by the acre.
L6-SOLO-02.week_en: Run weeding robots in fields on each farm's schedule. Bill by the acre. Maintain equipment and add clients by season.
L6-SOLO-02.entry_time_en: 3-6 months (buy or rent equipment plus first farms)
L6-SOLO-02.barrier_en: High equipment capital to buy, or rental needed. Strongly seasonal.
L6-SOLO-02.risk_en: Farms buy their own equipment. Vendor direct RaaS squeezes out middleman contractors.
L6-SOLO-02.next_en: 12-24 months to a regional agricultural robot service company.

L6-SOLO-03.do_en: Buy autonomous floor scrubbing robots. Take on commercial cleaning contracts. The robots replace large amounts of manual floor work.
L6-SOLO-03.week_en: Deploy robots to contract sites for night cleaning. By day, inspect, maintain, and find new contracts. Bill by area or contract.
L6-SOLO-03.barrier_en: Machine capital required, or RaaS rental.
L6-SOLO-03.risk_en: Vendor direct RaaS bypasses solo operators. Cleaning prices fall in a price war.
L6-SOLO-03.next_en: 12-24 months to a regional cleaning robot RaaS (L6-MICRO-01).

L6-MICRO-01.do_en: A team of 1-5 resells and integrates regional robot-as-a-service for cleaning, security, and service robots. Work covers on-site deployment, training, account management, and preventive maintenance.
L6-MICRO-01.week_en: Monday to Tuesday, channel sales and financing plans. Wednesday to Thursday, deploy, train, and maintain. Friday, manage accounts, hold SLAs, and grow the team.
L6-MICRO-01.barrier_en: Needs channel relationships, service capability, and working capital.
L6-MICRO-01.risk_en: Vendors selling direct bypass resellers. RaaS prices compress.
L6-MICRO-01.next_en: 12-24 months to a regional robot deployment service company.

L6-MICRO-02.name_en: Vertical Robot Adoption Consultant for SMBs
L6-MICRO-02.do_en: A team of 1-5 helps small businesses in food, warehousing, and cleaning adopt robots. Services cover needs assessment, ROI modeling, vendor selection, deployment PM, and change management.
L6-MICRO-02.week_en: Monday to Tuesday, client diagnosis and ROI modeling. Wednesday to Thursday, vendor selection and deployment PM. Friday, track results and manage staff change.
L6-MICRO-02.barrier_en: Needs industry contacts and consulting credibility.
L6-MICRO-02.risk_en: Vendors offer free deployment consulting. RaaS lowers decision complexity.
L6-MICRO-02.next_en: 12-24 months to a vertical robot integration or consulting firm.

L6-MICRO-03.name_en: Construction / Infrastructure Drone Service Company (1-5 people)
L6-MICRO-03.do_en: A drone service company of 1-5 people. Serves construction and infrastructure clients on a retainer model. Delivers recurring mapping, inspection, and data reports.
L6-MICRO-03.week_en: Monday, schedule multi-site flights. Tuesday to Thursday, the team flies and captures data. Friday, process data, report, manage retainers, and find clients.
L6-MICRO-03.entry_time_en: 1-3 months (build team, equipment, and first retainers)
L6-MICRO-03.barrier_en: Needs several certified pilots, equipment, and client contracts.
L6-MICRO-03.risk_en: Fully autonomous docked drones, such as Skydio Dock, replace manual service.
L6-MICRO-03.next_en: 12-24 months to a regional drone or geospatial data company.
